source: US Special Envoy / ACLED Modeling / UN
note: Estimates range from 27,000 (UN verified) to 400,000 (US envoy high estimate). ACLED satellite modeling for Khartoum alone: 61,000+ deaths Apr 2023–Jun 2024. 90% of deaths go unrecorded.
UN direct count; widely acknowledged as severe undercount. Covers only verifiable deaths where data reached UN systems. Most deaths from Sudan's blackout zones never reach official records.
Satellite imagery and mortality modeling estimated 26,000+ intentional-injury deaths and 61,000+ all-cause deaths in Khartoum, a 50% increase over prewar mortality rate.

category: El Geneina / West Darfur Massacre Deaths
note: Deaths in El Geneina from April to November 2023. Sudanese Red Crescent counted 2,000 bodies before stopping. UN Security Council report estimated 10,000–15,000 total killed, predominantly Massalit civilians.
RSF and Janjaweed massacred Massalit, Tama, and Erenga civilians in Ardamata neighborhood; ethnically targeted killings with mass sexual violence. One of the war's worst single-day atrocities.

RSF massacres after capture of El Fasher; primarily Zaghawa and Fur ethnic communities. UN FFM characterized as 'hallmarks of genocide.' Worst single war crime of the entire conflict per humanitarian experts.
RSF assault on Zamzam IDP camp (500,000 residents) in April 2025. Majority of victims were women and children. WFP had paused food distributions to the camp weeks before the assault.
MSF reported a child dying every 2 hours in Zamzam camp at peak famine conditions. 4.9 million children under 5 acutely malnourished. Experts believe starvation deaths exceed combat deaths.

At least 22 aid workers killed since April 2023, including staff from MSF, WFP, UNHCR, and national NGOs. Many more injured or kidnapped. Sudan became one of the world's most dangerous humanitarian operations.

400+ women sought medical care for sexual violence in first 16 months; actual numbers vastly higher due to stigma, inaccessibility, and lack of healthcare. Both RSF and SAF forces implicated; RSF bears greater documented responsibility.
9.3 million IDPs within Sudan as of December 2024; world's largest internal displacement crisis. Over 6 million are children. Most displaced from Khartoum, Darfur, and Gezira states.

2.5+ million Sudanese refugees in Chad (600,000+), Egypt (700,000+), South Sudan (400,000+), Ethiopia, and Libya. 90%+ of Darfur refugees arriving in Chad are women and children; 1 in 5 children arriving are acutely malnourished.

145+ healthcare facilities looted, destroyed, or rendered non-functional since April 2023. 65% of Darfur's population deprived of basic medical services. Multiple hospitals occupied by armed fighters.
